entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-30 23:06PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Victoria and NSW could lose hundreds of millions in GST; PM’s trade talks with EU to be fast-tracked; Russian forces leave Snake Island
  • ‘You’re back’: Emmanuel Macron singles out Tanya Plibersek at oceans summit
  • US court’s environment agency ruling a problem for the entire planet
  • ‘Won’t stop believing’: Beveridge backing Bulldogs on hard road ahead
  • Strictly Ballroom and Romper Stomper changed cinema. What do they say about us?
  • Cummins hits one of the biggest sixes of all time
  • Hamilton pushes back against "old voices" in F1
  • Cotton buds, helium balloons on banned plastics list, with coffee cups in sight
  • Worst start in history: Recession panic wipes $19 trillion off markets
  • Queen Elizabeth beams as she makes second Scotland appearance
  • Sydney commuters in for delayed, crowded and cancelled trains
  • President Ferdinand Marcos jnr sworn in, sings his father’s praises
  • Food blogger praised for response to fat-shamer at cake shop
  • Honours just about even after late wickets at Galle
  • No excuse for so many of Test cricket’s dark days
  • How could Putin’s Ukraine war trigger famine more than 8000km away?
  • ‘Bull crap’: Australian bats abandon old orthodoxy
  • Women, young and low-paid workers winners from super boost
  • ‘Four minutes of mayhem’: DCE masterclass as Eagles rock Storm before late flourish
  • Aussies power ahead from the eye of a storm
  • Carbon sanctions still on the table as Australia pursues EU trade deal
  • Falling population to cost NSW and Victoria cash and possibly seats in parliament
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • $2.1 billion Guardian-class patrol boats for Pacific island nations have major flaws
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • Best of cartoons, July 1, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, June 30, 2022
  • Skills mismatch drives job vacancies to nearly half a million
  • Butler orders swift inquiry into COVID-19 vaccine purchasing
  • Cartoons of the week
  • The multimillion-dollar Clovelly home that’s like Dr Who’s Tardis
  • Claims of no ‘catastrophic’ flood plan for Lismore despite decades-old warning
  • Women lawyers outnumber men in NSW but earn less at every age
  • Major disruptions for Sydney rail network as unions push ahead with action
  • Guy Sebastian’s former manager Titus Day found guilty of embezzling his money
  • ‘I was under a lot of pressure’: Councillor’s wife denies giving false evidence to ICAC
  • Lismore nursing home left to fend for itself as flood inundated premises
  • ‘She took off’: Dawson’s claim in phone tap about missing wife Lynette
  • Public and Catholic teachers rally in historic joint strike
  • NSW teachers' strike
  • William Tyrrell’s foster mother accused of hitting child with wooden spoon, court hears
  • Barilaro inquired about NY trade job before public ads, as NSW minister calls for him to withdraw
  • Tens of thousands of striking teachers march in Sydney
  • Relentless rain in northeast India causes another deadly mudslide
  • Russian forces leave Snake Island, keep up eastern assault
  • Biden slams ‘destabilising’ Supreme Court, works to codify Roe v Wade
  • Endgame nears for Trump as chaotic cadre unravels
  • China insulted by NATO ‘threat’ label, Russia reacts to alliance expansion
  • Sole surviving member of Paris bombings to spend life in prison
  • Carrie Lam’s banquet for Xi Jinping cancelled due to rampant COVID
  • R Kelly sentenced for sex trafficking
  • Failed test a fresh setback for US hypersonic missile program
  • ‘I’m Penny Wong and I was born here’: Australia’s new soft power in Asia
  • Beer made with recycled sewage a plus for Singapore
  • The killing of Emmett Till
  • Prince Andrew must be the next target for FBI, say Epstein victims
  • British MPs may delay Australia trade deal after minister’s no-show
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘Violence, cruelty and control’: R. Kelly jailed for 30 years for sex trafficking
  • Karl ribbed over classic Dalai Lama joke
  • Airport mayhem ahead of holidays
  • Train strike to continue today
  • New gang task force for Victoria
  • We need to have a talk about Reconciliation and what it means
  • Climate-driven food shocks are coming, but we can mitigate the risks
  • Australian man dies at resort in Thailand
  • South Australia records first monkeypox case
  • Family of Queensland mum missing in Mexico clings to hope
  • Former judge to probe Star’s Qld casinos with public hearing powers
  • As it happened: John Barilaro quits New York trade role; Victorians get $250 if they shop around on energy; Albanese to meet with French president
  • WA facing 'extreme' egg shortage
  • NATO labels China a security threat
  • John Barilaro withdraws from New York trade role
  • ASX eyes rise despite Wall Street losses to close worst first-half since 1970; Bitcoin tumbles
  • Four-day work week has a chance to gain traction this time
  • Is it OK to grump about the latest corporate values push?
  • ‘Cryptoqueen’ added to FBI’s most-wanted list after alleged $5.8b fraud
  • Star casino can’t play James Packer’s ace
  • Australia hasn’t had the Great Resignation, but more people are quitting now
  • Despite being ‘fraught with risk’, this lawyer thinks Web3 has legs
  • The Wrap: ASX ends financial year 10.2 per cent lower after dramatic plunge
  • Gig economy drivers need a legislated safety net
  • Seven moves to tear up Cricket Australia deal over alleged Big Bash League breaches
  • Stranger things: Brookfield’s cover blown as battle for AGL takes thrilling turn
  • Watchdog greenlights Australia’s most polluting project to run to 2070
  • Abercrombie to chair Humm once again after other directors resign

  • Intrigue as Brookfield acquires stake in AGL after failed takeover bid
  • Elon Musk’s tweets echo the tactics used by Donald Trump
  • ‘Those with money will be fine’: Crypto crash is exposing its great divide
  • ‘Lazy and indulgent’: Arts minister slams previous government’s board appointments

  • George Clooney and Julia Roberts channel Mamma Mia! in Aussie-filmed rom-com

  • Trailer: Ticket to Paradise
  • What is √9 + 2 (3 x 2)? How Eddie Woo taught me to like maths again

  • Why I couldn’t help falling in love with Austin Butler’s Elvis
  • The Resort official trailer
  • Official trailer for Ticket to Paradise
  • From the Archives, 1932: 90 Years of the ABC
  • The world gets worse, but public libraries are forever
  • National weather forecast for Friday July 1

  • NSW forests still face chainsaw despite emission reduction targets
  • Race to stop spread of deadly mite found in Australian bee hives for first time
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Washington Post: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine live updates: Biden pledges $800 million in new weapons for Ukraine, says U.S. gas prices will remain high

  • Putin is forcing Germans to get real about the military

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: new ‘iron curtain’ between Russia and west, Kremlin says, as Nato leaders pledge support to Kyiv – live

  • Guns N’ Roses – their 20 greatest songs, ranked!
  • Hong Kong tightens security as Xi visits for 25th anniversary of handover
  • Pushing Buttons: Happy 50th birthday to Atari, whose simple games gave us so much
  • Twenty-six seconds of fame: how Doctor Strange got upstaged by a swivel-eyed extra
  • Ben & Jerry’s criticises deal that will resume sales in occupied territories
  • The discovery of the polio virus in Britain proves we can never let our guard down | Andrew Pollard
  • Dining across the divide: ‘He was not a Nigel Farage-type Brexit person, so that was nice’
  • Nick Cave & Warren Ellis: Seven Psalms review – intimate prayers of extreme power
  • ‘Blood money’: Europe’s secretive trade in Syrian phosphates
  • Is Stranger Things even TV any more?

  • Ancient pharaohs, weird science and lovely Liza Minnelli – take the Thursday quiz
  • Denise Richards: ‘If there’s a lost dog in the neighbourhood, they will always find me’

  • The first time I’ve enjoyed cycling in a city: my Tour de Copenhagen
  • Fossil fuel industry faces surge in climate lawsuits

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 30 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-30 22:37PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Monumental’ call: Hong Kong can be China’s international I&T hub, Xi Jinping says on visit to city’s Science Park
  • Ballistic briefcases, machine guns and a bulletproof car: the unprecedented security blanket thrown around Hong Kong for Xi Jinping’s visit
  • ‘Risen from the ashes’: Xi Jinping hails Hong Kong’s resilience, praises vitality of ‘one country, two systems’
  • China’s first lady Peng Liyuan charms young Hong Kong opera performers during visit to Xiqu Centre
  • Lockdowns, a lion dance and red carpet: how Hong Kong welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping
  • Coronavirus outbreak in Hong Kong shifting from pandemic to endemic, says government health adviser
  • Hong Kong protests: kitchen worker receives longest jail time so far for rioting and breaching mask ban during Wan Chai demonstration in 2019
  • CityU awards 49 local start-ups up to HK$1million each under HK Tech 300 Angel Fund
  • Sino Land sells all 238 flats at Lohas Park in three hours as buyers rush to get ahead of rising mortgage rates
  • BN(O) scheme: London mayor underlines support for Hong Kong people who move to the capital
  • Hong Kong police arrest alleged driver of vehicle seen travelling against traffic in dashcam video posted online
  • As it happened: Chinese leader Xi Jinping hails ‘vitality’ of ‘one country, two systems’ in arrival speech in Hong Kong, endorses it as a ‘good system’
  • More than a tropical island: Phuket residents discover international school that offers world-recognised holistic ‘education in paradise’
  • China’s art-market rebound gives way to mood of uncertainty as economic challenges mount
  • In 1 word: how 25 people describe Hong Kong, a city close to their hearts
  • Hong Kong issues No 3 typhoon warning signal as tropical storm Chaba nears, with President Xi Jinping visiting for 25th anniversary of city’s return to Chinese rule
  • Hong Kong U-turns on tightening pollution rules for classic cars, keeping exemptions for vehicles aged 30 years or more
  • What Chinese President Xi Jinping told Hong Kong in 2017 and key messages he can be expected to deliver on 25th anniversary of handover
  • Unofficial US-China dialogue: seeing the worst in each other undermines nations’ already dismal relations
  • China urged to boost defence talks with US, Japan, South Korea as trio joins hands on Pyongyang nuclear threat
  • Tech war: China doubles down on domestic operating systems to cut reliance on Windows, MacOS from the US
  • President Xi Jinping’s visit to a laser equipment maker highlights government’s continued focus on tech self-sufficiency
  • US-Taiwan trade talks rankle mainland China as ‘inevitable’ deal seen pulling island into US orbit
  • How long have pandas been eating bamboo? At least 6 million years, study suggests
  • Tencent plays up ‘social value’ of video gaming tech as China’s regulators continue scrutiny of content the sector creates
  • Gunmen kidnap 4 Chinese workers from Nigeria mine
  • Overseas flights in high demand as Shanghai resumes international travel after lockdown
  • BA.4 and BA.5: what we know about the new Omicron subvariants
  • China stalls on EU trade talks date as push for Lithuania WTO case gains ground, bloc officials say
  • US-listed Full Truck Alliance and Kanzhun resume user registrations in sign of China’s cybersecurity probes nearing end
  • US firms in China praise quarantine cut, but coronavirus restrictions still weigh heavy on confidence
  • China’s Communist Party nears 97 million, with more younger and educated members
  • Climate Change: Chinese offshore oil and gas giant eyes major clean energy output expansion to help nation reach environmental goals
  • JD.com extends access to Tencent’s WeChat for three years with US$220 million in stock
  • PLA soldiers train with rapid-fire mortar in video for Hong Kong handover anniversary
  • Webb telescope allows Nasa to reveal the deepest image ever taken of the universe
  • Chinese team implants brain sensor without cracking skull
  • China’s economic recovery clouded by zero-Covid even as manufacturing, services expand for first time in 4 months
  • Fewer billionaires in mainland China as ultra-wealthy feel pain of Beijing’s tech, property crackdowns, study says
  • Indonesian President Joko Widodo says he gave Vladimir Putin message from Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky
  • New Pacific alliance PBP to counter China’s influence will likely grow to include more European nations, say analysts
  • Japan proposes joint custody amid rise in divorce, reluctance from legal sector
  • Coronavirus: South Korea approves first home-made Covid-19 vaccine
  • Marcos Jnr sworn in as Philippine president decades after father was ousted from power
  • How tropical islands in Indonesia and Thailand are the new working from home
  • North Korea’s woes overflow as Kim Jong-un battles flooding, Covid-19 outbreak
  • New Zealand Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern warns Nato of China’s rising assertiveness
  • NYPD unit that inspired ‘Law & Order: SVU’ faces probe for gender bias
  • UK lawmaker who ‘drank far too much’ quits government amid groping reports
  • Ketanji Brown Jackson sworn in as first black woman on US Supreme Court
  • Hells Angels founder Sonny Barger dead at 83
  • World’s biggest chocolate plant in Belgium shut after salmonella outbreak
  • Russian troops withdraw from Ukraine’s Snake Island in ‘goodwill gesture’
  • Putin hits back at Johnson’s woman remarks, points to Thatcher’s Falklands war
  • Cassidy Hutchinson: The ex-Trump White House aide with a ‘smoking gun’
  • ‘High on meth’ truck driver pretended to be victim after Texas migrant deaths
  • ‘Noisy’ protester silenced outside UK parliament as police get new powers
  • How leaders mingle after Nato meetings: with fine food, art and music at Madrid’s famous Prado Museum
  • Google’s ‘fast track to surveillance’ sparks backlash from European consumer groups over ‘deceptive’ practices
  • Putin says invasion going ‘to plan’ as Russia fights to encircle Ukraine’s last eastern stronghold
  • Why zero-Covid China is looking more appealing to global investors
  • Climate change: Only 11 per cent of listed firms globally on track to achieve key carbon-emissions goal, MSCI says
  • EU reaches agreement on crypto regulation requiring personal data collection on every transfer
  • Dynamic change may be in the past for an ageing Hong Kong amid greater mainland integration
  • Hong Kong stocks log best month since October on China outlook while Tencent, SenseTime tumble as insiders sell
  • NYSE-listed Chinese low-cost retailer Miniso targets US$116 million in secondary Hong Kong float
  • Carousell rides sustainability trend in Hong Kong, aims to make buying and selling of second-hand items ‘as frictionless as possible’
  • Alibaba’s cloud services unit launches carbon management tool for its global business customers to aid decarbonisation drive
  • Coal-rich, gas-poor China needs more carbon capture and energy storage tech for new power system, analysts say
  • After durian whiskey comes crab whiskey from a US distillery known for eclectic craft spirits
  • Burning movie review: Fatal Attraction rip-off starring Kevin Cheng and Dada Chan is tame and thoroughly unimaginative
  • Kourtney Kardashian ate her placenta and called it life-changing. Should you eat yours? Experts weigh in
  • African Fashion show at V&A in London marks museum’s push to reverse its exclusion and misrepresentation of continent’s creativity
  • Urban farmer who set out to make dining more sustainable in Hong Kong on bringing the ‘magical’ taste of fresh produce to the city
  • 5 healthy habits that are trending across Hong Kong in 2022
  • Chinese tourists missing out as global tourism resumes – will they ever return in numbers again?
  • Liposuction, nose job abroad: why people travel for cosmetic surgery and the factors to consider
  • Son Ye-jin is pregnant! 5 reasons why she’ll be the perfect mum to her first child with new husband Hyun Bin, from her K-drama roles as strong women to her cooking skills shown off on Instagram
  • Meet Elton John’s sons, Zachary and Elijah: Lady Gaga is their godmother and David Beckham was once asked to give them football tips, but will they follow in dad’s showbiz footsteps after he retires?
  • Meet Nico Santos, set to star in Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3: the Filipino-American LGBT actor was a part-time waiter before he rose to fame in NBC’s Superstore and Crazy Rich Asians
  • 5 Hong Kong celebrities who look amazing at age 60: Tony Leung, Stephen Chow, Rosamund Kwan, Pansy Ho and Alex To all hit the milestone birthday in 2022 – but aren’t showing it a bit
  • How Kenzo found a preppy, 70s groove at Paris Fashion Week: Nigo’s autumn/winter 2022 menswear collection mixed Scout’s scarves with African vibrancy and Asian crossover styles
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Shiga area tries to keep school for Brazilians afloat during pandemic
  • Early pressure patterns causing record-setting heat in Japan
  • Thermal plant problem causes power outage scare in Tokyo

  • South Korea approves first homemade COVID-19 vaccine
  • Japan’s Kirin gives up stake in Myanmar Brewery

  • Russia and China slam NATO after alliance raises alarm
  • New Zealand PM says China more assertive, more willing to challenge rules
  • TASTE OF LIFE/ Low-salt miso soup: Make a savory soup that cuts down on the salt, not the flavor
  • EDITORIAL: Legal systems for marriage, family should match societal changes
  • VOX POPULI: GPS functions on our devices have rusted over the mind’s compass
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
